reviews.review_description:
I played the demo a while ago and while I loved the atmosphere and art direction there were some problems with the gameplay.

Some issues like the floaty jumps have been addressed but the game still doesn't feel polished and the further I get into it the more problems arise.

The core gameplay is lifted directly from Moss which bothers me a little bit. I wouldn't be surprised if it bothers Polyarc a bit more, although since "imitation is the sincerest form of flattery" maybe Polyarc's cool with it.

I love the Moss games so I was happy to give this a second try. I'm also a big fan of this developer's earlier game UNBINARY.

Unfortunately the gameplay in My Monsters has some serious issues, the primary one being combat. They didn't lift Moss' ability to grab an enemy to give your character a fighting chance so it's all up to the character's own abilities.  This is fine in the beginning, but as the game progresses there are situations where the enemies have an unfair advantage. There are areas where you need to go behind the scenery to progress. There's the standard "shadow icon" to show where your character is behind the scenery but no indicators for the enemies. The enemies are bullet sponges (claw sponges?), so you can't just blindly swipe at the unseen enemies to get past these areas. I had to walk into the game and past the scenery once to get through a tunnel. Good luck getting past these areas if you have to play seated. There are also open areas that have sections blocked by scenery where both your character and the enemies are invisible.  This is just poor game design and very frustrating.

There aren’t much in the way of puzzles, the gameplay consists primarily of fetch quests which I actually don’t mind at all, but this may be a negative for some.

There are a few technical glitches.

Occasionally a dialog box for a character stays open and you cannot close it without walking over to the character to hit the “x” button.  This has happened when revisiting an area and with the dialog box blocking much of the screen it can be difficult to navigate. You can easily fall to your death in this game, so being forced to fumble along while blinded by a broken dialog box is very aggravating.

The narrator's audio bunches up so bits are lost. It's like the speech is made up of short clips played in sequence and some clips are playing too soon and cutting the previous clip short.  This isn’t an infrequent glitch, it happens nearly every time the narrator speaks.

One dialog box had more text than could fit so it bunched up and the lines of text overlapped each other vertically. 

There’s a pathway that suddenly became impassable.  I’m hoping I’m not required to backtrack to that area to complete the game.

I’m very disappointed by the gameplay, but not sorry I bought it.  I really do love the art direction and I’m determined to slog through the entire game to see all of it.

The art style is fantastic. The closer you look at the scene you start to find little hidden creatures and funny Easter eggs. It’s worth it to spend a little extra time looking over the entire diorama (not just the play space). I enjoy the neon color palette. 
    The gameplay grows in difficulty. It seems almost too simple at first- which is perfect for a player that’s new to VR platformers- but it builds quite nicely and I’m even finding the combat a bit challenging. It’s requiring me to strategize and experiment a bit before I can beat some of the “arenas”. The mechanics are very similar to the Moss game so it was easy to catch on if you’ve played those before. 
    The story is cute. And i have not found any bugs/ glitches in may play through.

Coming from someone who suffers from anxiety, this game has a lot of purpose and meaning.  The hand painted world is gorgeous, the music and voice acting are great, and the mechanics are solid.  I look forward to seeing this through to the credits.  Great job devs, you made something totally different from what's out there in vr, and it should be rewarded.  It's worth every penny!!

developer_response.body: It really means a lot to us to hear that the game resonated with you, especially given its themes. We wanted My Monsters to be a meaningful experience about facing what’s inside us, so knowing it connected in that way makes it all worth it! Thanks for the review!

reviews.review_description: My Monsters is an unforgettable VR experience. The hand-painted art is stunning, the combat feels smooth and satisfying, and the world is full of charm and personality. What surprised me most is how emotionally immersive it is. I actually teared up at the ending. Few VR games manage to balance fun gameplay with such heartfelt storytelling, but this one pulls it off beautifully. If you want a game that looks gorgeous, plays great, and leaves a lasting impression, My Monsters is a must-play.

developer_response.body:
Wow, thanks so much for your review and for sharing your experience in such a personal and heartfelt way! 
We’re thrilled to hear that My Monsters resonated with you on such an emotional level, it’s incredibly rewarding for us. Reading your words genuinely made our day!
Thank you for being part of this journey with us!

reviews.review_title: Very fun diorama style game great art
reviews.review_description: This game does a lot right from the get go. The first thing you will notice is the art style. It is amazing hand drawn style you don't see a lot in quest games. It has a little bit for everyone, puzzle, platform and combat. The story in the game is also unique and I won't get too much into it as I don't want to spoil it. If you like games like moss you will definitely love this game and I recommend it!

developer_response.body:
We’re so thrilled you enjoyed the game!
The hand-drawn art style was one of our biggest passions, and it’s wonderful to hear it stood out to you. Moss was definitely an inspiration, but we’re so glad My Monsters was able to offer its own unique mix of puzzles, platforming, combat, and story. Thanks for the recommendation, it means a lot!

reviews.review_description:
My Monsters is an action-adventure VR game that with a 100% hand-painted art, creating a visually stunning world full of fantasy and surrealism! It’s absolutely amazing! 

At the heart of the game is a deep narrative that explores humanity's greatest inner struggles—anxiety, fear, and loneliness! Im sure we can all relate to this at some point in our lives! As a player, you are transported to Onirium, a dream-like realm where you meet Moti, a frightened little monster with no memory ( so cute ). 

You’re never alone! Team up with your inner monster, Moti, to face a variety of enemies, solve intricate puzzles, and overcome challenges in a shared journey. This partnership mechanic fosters a fantastic & powerful connection with Moti, requiring empathy and courage to navigate obstacles and reveal fragments of a meaningful dreamlike tale. Every stage introduces new aspects of this bond, allowing players to experience moments of self-discovery and emotional depth.

I recommend this to be in your games library I’ve had a blast playing it! Well done devs stunning game.

developer_response.body:
Wow, we’re so happy you had such a great time with My Monsters! 
Means a lot to hear that the bond with Moti and the world of Onirium really came through. We poured so much love into the hand-painted art and the story, and knowing it touched you personally makes all the late nights worth it. 
Thanks for playing and for sharing your experience, it truly warms our hearts!

reviews.review_title: Adventure You’ll Never Want to Leave
reviews.review_description:
From the very first moment, My Monsters wins you over with its enchanting art style and warm, magical atmosphere. You’re instantly drawn into a world full of wonder, where every environment feels alive and every encounter adds a touch of charm and curiosity. The attention to detail and the studio’s distinctive signature style really shine — check out their updates on the Meta Store to see how far they’ve refined their craft.

This is a Moss-like experience, but with its own unique soul. The controls are fluid and intuitive, the dialogue is engaging, and the emotional depth gives real meaning to your journey. Whether you’re a platformer fan or just love well-crafted adventures, My Monsters is a must-play experience that stays with you long after the credits roll.

⭐️ 5/5 – A delightful, heartfelt journey into a monster’s world.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this review!
We’re really happy that the experience and atmosphere resonated with you! Moss was indeed a big inspiration for us, and hearing that My Monsters found its own soul means a lot!

reviews.review_description: It's one of those gems that no one talks about but that you absolutely mustn't miss. My Monsters will blow you away with its narration and its unparalleled artistic touch. Lasting between 4 and 5 hours, you will travel through this dreamlike world filled with color and secrets in a platformer style without ever ceasing to amaze you.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! We’re thrilled that the narration, art, and dreamlike world of My Monsters resonated with you.

reviews.review_description:
Gorgeous game combat is fun and the graphics are amazing to look at story telling is very well done highly recommend 👏
Even though it'll play to similar games you play before this story is very top written and very good kinda wish there was more dialog instead of reading text but it doesn't hurt the fact that your getting a full product and finished game. I feel for my monster and ill hope more people would give this game a try so we can see what else the developers can make in the future!!!!!

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you!
We’re so happy you enjoyed My Monsters. 

It means a lot that you connected with Moti, and we hope even more players get to experience the game and the world of Onirium. Your support really inspires us to keep creating!

reviews.review_description: I was so excited to play this game and the first level is fantastic, the second awards the end there were a couple glitches,but the third level it is terrible with glitches. The first two levels super easy, but the third with not being able to fight and only shoot at times is making it almost impossible to win, and the game not letting you have to go out and restart due to it just staying on loading page,made me have to restart a level. The game itself is beautiful, and the story is great. Just that third level glitches is a pain.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your feedback!

We’re aware of issues in some parts of the game and want you to know that our team is actively working on a fix. The update addressing these glitches will be released very soon and should significantly improve the gameplay experience.

We really appreciate your patience and are sorry for the problems you encountered.

reviews.review_description: There is a glitch and it won't let me go past the double hearts even after i defeated them been waiting on a update no reply waste of my money to bad was a cute game

developer_response.body:
Hi Uniquela,

We’re really sorry about that bug. This is not the experience we want you to have, and we truly appreciate you letting us know. 

The good news is that we just released an update that fixes this issue, so you should now be able to progress past the double hearts map.

Thank you for your patience and support!

reviews.review_description:
So far only played the demo and I usually don't spend more than $10 on games but this was worth it. I suffer severely from anxiety and panic attacks. But I refuse to take medication. I have found vr to be a really big help and this is one of those games that really helped. Thanks to the devs for being so thoughtful. Also they have another game called urinary: AI awakening which is really cool. I love the hand painted graphic design and hopefully you guys come out with more games. I'm a fan at this point and that says a lot.


Edit: ok I see a lot of reviews comparing my monsters to moss but honestly I don't see the connection. I have played moss 1 and 2. I  have to say my monsters is in a category all by itself. Hands down no comparison to other vr games we have played and we have over 500 games in our library. I think that says a lot.

I've been playing this game since beta testing. There were some bugs in the beginning, but the hand-drawn artistic graphics and fantastic story were an impressive experience that's different from mass-produced games. Indy developer spent a long time communicating with beta testers to complete the game to get rid of the bugs. It's a 3d flatformer dungeon game similar to MOSS, but the difficulty level is not so hard and you'll immerse yourself in a fresh story and keep going. I highly recommend it.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this review and for being part of the beta testing journey!
Your feedback and patience truly helped us shape My Monsters into what it is today. Thanks for recommending it and for believing in our team!

reviews.review_description:
My Monsters is a complete masterpiece!
The world, the characters, the soundtrack, and the atmosphere are all beautiful and carefully crafted. Every level feels unique, and the game always gave me the feeling of exploring something special.

I’ve been following and playtesting this project for a while, and it’s amazing to see how far it has come. The developers clearly put a lot of love into this game, and it really shows.

If you like storytelling adventures that have heart, creativity, and charm, this is a must-play. Highly recommended!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for such a heartfelt review!
It truly means a lot to us, especially coming from someone who’s been following the project from early on. Seeing players connect with the world, the characters, and the atmosphere the way you did makes all the hard work worthwhile.
And by the way, we love your profile picture!

reviews.review_description: Platform game ...with a soul! The design is unique, every stage is a piece of art, like playing in a living paint full of colours .Good gameplay, great story that will keep you engaged. This game is worth every penny. Just buy and you won't be disappointed. Well done developers!!!!!!❤️❤️❤️

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you so much for your review!
Creating a “living painting” full of colors and emotion was our dream, and it means so much to know you felt it. Thanks for supporting Moti’s adventure and for your kind words, they truly make the team proud!

reviews.review_description:
If I were a DEV, I could not have written a more perfect game for myself. I love traveling through lands both magical and magnificent—the art is incredible and a real feast for the eyes—and I love that my adorable fellow traveler and I have a connection whereby we share and help each other. I am a big fan of Moss and Moss 2 and I could not be more delighted at the similarities in these games and the fact that My Monsters has nothing to do with shooting! I have been into VR for about 3 years and there seem to be at least 10 to 20 shooters for every other type of game, particularly one with such great and delightful graphics, not to mention the loveliness of the characters. I also enjoy another game by Ludact called Unbinary, but it is obvious that they have. Major success in My Monsters. So congratulations  to Ludact on Monsters—it is an awesome addition to my library that I will look forward to playing every day. 


Ludact

developer_response.body:
If you were a dev of My Monsters, you can be sure you’d be feeling just as happy reading your words! 

Thank you so much for this thoughtful and kind review. We’re also big fans of the games you mentioned , they were a huge inspiration, and we’re thrilled you enjoyed the similarities while also seeing the unique identity of My Monsters. 

We’re also so glad you’ve known us since Unbinary! Thank you for your continued support, it truly means a lot to us!
